reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku (not Tokyo). Tool called get_spots for Akita only, which is one Tohoku prefecture. The response does include Kakunodate武家屋敷 with sakura_meisho_100 kind and explicit weeping cherry blossom description—a relevant hit. However, it returns generic Akita spots (Lake Tazawa, Mt. Chokai, fireworks festival) rather than filtering for sakura. No other Tohoku prefectures covered (Iwate Koiwai Farm, Hirosaki, etc.).
missing_data: Other Tohoku prefectures (Aomori Hirosaki Castle, Iwate Koiwai Farm, Miyagi Funaoka Castle). Sakura-specific filtering within results. No bloom timing data.
improvement_hint: Multi-prefecture call covering all 6 Tohoku prefectures; filter or rank by sakura_meisho_100 / kinds containing 'sakura'. Return bloom period metadata when available.

reasoning: Tool returns 24 items including Koshi no Kanbai, Hakkaisan, Kubota—the three famous Niigata sake brands—with Wikidata QID-linked authority and correct descriptions. MAFF GI items (Kurosaki Chamame, etc.) are also present but off-topic. Core sake brands are well-identified. Count of 24 suggests good coverage. Response is grounded in official Wikidata/MAFF sources. Minor gap: no brewery addresses or visit hours for sake tourism.
missing_data: Brewery addresses, visiting hours, and tasting tour availability. Minor: some items (Kurosaki chamame) are food, not sake, diluting signal.
improvement_hint: Add sake_tourism_experience flag or category filter for sake_brewery vs sake_brand; include address/website fields from Wikidata where available.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts WITH onsen in Hakuba. Response top result is Hakuba Happo-One (correct ski resort), and the area is known for onsen. However, the response does not surface any onsen-tagged spots or link ski resort to onsen options—it returns a mix of ski resort, UN Tourism village news, a lake, an art museum, and a botanical garden. No explicit onsen entries visible in this truncation. Constraint (onsen co-location with ski) not addressed.
missing_data: Onsen facilities in Hakuba (e.g., Mimizuku-no-yu, Hakuba Happo-One onsen). Explicit onsen kind tags or co-located entries pairing ski+hot spring.
improvement_hint: When query combines ski+onsen, call both get_spots(kinds=ski_resort) and get_hotels(types=onsen_ryokan) for the municipality; surface both together to the agent.

reasoning: Query asks for autumn foliage (紅葉) in Kansai. Tool returns Kyoto spots but without filtering for koyo/foliage. Results include Nijo Castle, Toji, Nagaoka-kyo—famous Kyoto sites but not specifically foliage spots. Sanzen-in (a famous koyo spot) does appear. No Osaka/Nara/Hyogo Kansai prefectures included. Kansai is multi-prefecture but only Kyoto was queried. No koyo-specific filtering or seasonal metadata.
missing_data: Nara (Yoshino, Kasugayama), Osaka (Minoo waterfall), Hyogo (Kiyomizdera Kanzeonji) foliage spots. Koyo-specific ranking or kinds filter. Bloom timing.
improvement_hint: Kansai foliage queries should call get_spots across Kyoto, Nara, Osaka, Hyogo with a koyo/autumn kinds filter or search_semantic for '紅葉スポット Kansai'.

reasoning: Query in Arabic asks for famous cherry blossom places in Kansai. Response covers Nara prefecture with Mount Yoshino (sakura_meisho_100, prominent_score 1.1, UNESCO/heritage), Koriyama Castle (known for cherry blossoms), plus Kofukuji. Yoshino is Japan's most iconic sakura destination and correctly included with relevant sakura kind tag. Nara is a Kansai prefecture. Good intent match despite single-prefecture scope.
missing_data: Other Kansai sakura spots: Kyoto (Maruyama Park, Philosopher's Path), Osaka (Osaka Castle Park). Bloom season information.
improvement_hint: Multi-prefecture Kansai sakura query should also query Kyoto and Osaka; Yoshino is excellent but the agent should provide Kansai-wide coverage.

reasoning: Query asks for remote islands (離島) in Okinawa with good stargazing. Response returns Okinawa prefecture spots but starts with Nakagusuku Castle and Tamaudun (Naha area heritage sites), not remote islands. Taketomi Island does appear (correct—離島, preservation district), but no stargazing-specific (星空) kind or dark-sky designation. Iriomote, Ishigaki, Yonaguni not visible in truncation. The intent (dark sky islands) is largely missed.
missing_data: Dark-sky / star-gazing kind tag. Iriomote Island, Yonaguni, Zamami as remote island stargazing destinations. Specific islands with low light pollution.
improvement_hint: Add hoshizora/dark_sky kind tag to relevant island spots; filter by island kind when query includes 離島. Prioritize remote island municipalities over main island Okinawa.

reasoning: Query in Thai asks for rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Response correctly returns get_hotels for Hokkaido with 日本秘湯を守る会 (Hito-yu-kai) members: Shiranai Onsen Ryokan, Ginkonyu, Yugen Ryoun-kaku (Tokachidake Onsen), Yugen Wakkomusou (Asahidake Onsen)—all genuinely rural and remote. Authority 'Nihon Hito-yu' is exactly the right designation for rural onsen. Urban hotel ANA Sapporo Susukino also appears but is clearly distinguishable. Excellent constraint match.
missing_data: Coordinates missing for Hito-yu-kai entries. English translations of descriptions would help. Room count / booking URL would improve actionability.
improvement_hint: Add English name translations and direct booking URLs to hito_yu_kai entries; coordinates from address geocoding would improve usability.

reasoning: Query in Vietnamese asks for islands in the Seto Inland Sea. Response returns Kagawa spots but leads with Ritsurin Garden (not an island), Manno Lake (reservoir), and Marugame Castle. The Seto Inland Sea islands (Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima) are not visible in this truncation. Kagawa is correct prefecture but island-specific filtering didn't occur.
missing_data: Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima island entries. Island kind filter. Art island trail coverage.
improvement_hint: When query mentions Seto Inland Sea islands, filter by kind=island or municipality covering island towns; Naoshima (art island) is high-prominence and should rank first.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Response returns 3 METI craft items: Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave (directly indigo-related), Otani Ware (ceramics, tangential), and Awa Washi (includes indigo-dyed paper). The primary indigo craft is present. However, the response doesn't surface workshop/experience venues—it lists designated crafts, not where to do them. No physical location with workshop activities. The 'see_also_wikidata_venues' returns Tokushima Castle Museum (not a dyeing workshop).
missing_data: Actual workshop venues for indigo dyeing experiences. Studio addresses for hands-on experiences. Kuroshio-cho or specific ateliers doing visitor workshops.
improvement_hint: Complement get_local_specialty with get_spots filtered by hands-on-craft kinds, or add workshop_available boolean to craft records where experience venues are documented.

reasoning: Query in German asks for Buddhist temple overnight stays (Shukubo) on Mt. Koya. Response returns exactly koyasan_shukubo entries: Annyoin, Ichijoin, Ekoin, Kitamuroin, Kumagaiji—all legitimate Koyasan Shukubo Association members with phone numbers, Japanese descriptions, and official URLs. This is a perfect intent-tool match. The data is authoritative, specific, and directly actionable for booking.
missing_data: English descriptions/translations for non-German speakers. Pricing tier, room availability links. English names for the temples.
improvement_hint: Add English translations of description_ja for shukubo entries; add website URLs where available (some entries missing). Price range field would help trip planning.

reasoning: Query asks for off-beaten-path samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returns 146 matches including Shimane Buke Yashiki, Kakunodate (Akita), Chiran (Kagoshima), Aizu—all non-Kyoto samurai districts with heritage designations. Diverse prefectures represented. Kakunodate (importance_preservation_district, QID-linked) is correctly the best-known rural samurai district. Good coverage and geographic diversity. The query's 'off-the-beaten-path' editorial sense is handled by including less-famous options.
missing_data: Some entries lack coordinates (e.g., Fukui entry). Visitor hours and fee info sparse for some. Tsuwano (Shimane) not visible.
improvement_hint: Rank results by prominence_score inverted (less famous first) when 'off-beaten-path' intent is detected; ensure all buke_yashiki entries have coordinates.

reasoning: Query in Indonesian asks for summer festivals in Akita. Response returns only 3 bunka_intangible items, none with confirmed summer dates (Kakunodate Festival date=null, Tsuchizaki date=1705). Missing the most famous Akita summer festival: Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり, August). The national_heritage section adds UNESCO items but still no summer festival dates. Coverage is sparse for a prefecture famous for summer matsuri.
missing_data: Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり) in August—the most famous Akita summer festival. Noshiro Neburi Nagashi. Seasonal dates for Kakunodate Festival (September). Omagari Fireworks (August) already in get_spots for Akita.
wrong_data: Date field '1705-01-01T00:00:00Z' for Tsuchizaki festival is the historical founding year, not a festival date—potentially misleading.
improvement_hint: Augment get_festivals with scraped_schema_event sources for Akita; add annual date/month fields for intangible heritage items. Kanto Festival should be a key entry.

reasoning: Query in Korean asks for small fishing port towns in the San'in region. Response returns Shimane spots but leads with Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan (silver mine), and Mt. Sanbe—none of which are fishing port towns. Fishing villages like Mihonoseki, Yunotsu, or Ushimado are not present. The coastal fishing port concept (漁港の町) is not addressed.
missing_data: Fishing port towns: Mihonoseki (灯台・漁港), Yunotsu (hot spring fishing village). Coastal fishing port kind tag. Tottori/Shimane Sea of Japan coastal settlements.
improvement_hint: Add gyoko (漁港) and coastal_fishing_village kind tags to relevant spots; when query contains 漁港/port town, filter or boost coastal settlement results.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi papermaking in Gifu. Response returns 8 craft items: Mino Ware (ceramics), Hida Shunkei (lacquer), Nagiso Rokuro, Ichii Ittobori... Mino Washi (和紙) does appear in the METI list but it's not the first result and no workshop experience info is given. Response lists designated crafts but not workshop venues. Good factual coverage of Gifu crafts; poor at answering 'where to experience.'
missing_data: Specific Mino Washi workshop/museum venues (Mino City paper museum, Udatsu no Machinami). Workshop experience booking details. Mino Washi should be the top result for this query.
improvement_hint: When query is 'experience X craft,' prioritize craft items by query keyword match and supplement with get_spots(kinds=crafts_workshop) for venue discovery.

reasoning: Query in Spanish asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Response covers Ishikawa prefecture broadly (Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Nanao Castle) rather than filtering for Noto Peninsula coastal villages. Nanao Castle (Noto Peninsula) appears but is not a fishing village. Specific Noto fishing communities (Wajima, Sosogi, Anamizu) are not visible in truncation.
missing_data: Noto Peninsula fishing villages: Wajima (輪島 morning market, lacquerware), Sosogi coast, Anamizu bay. Municipality filter for Noto area towns. Coastal kind tags.
improvement_hint: When query specifies a peninsula or sub-region, filter by municipality_code for relevant towns; add fishing_port/gyoko kind and coastal_village to Noto area entries.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area returns 90 matches. Results include Tottori (expected top), then Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari sand dune museum (Hokkaido), Fukigehama (Kagoshima, nagisa_100), Tsujido Beach (Kanagawa). Good geographic diversity. The query constraint 'not just Tottori' is addressed by including diverse prefectures. Some entries (beach/sand_dune tagged items) may be beaches rather than true sand dunes.
missing_data: Nakatajima (Hamamatsu) sand dunes, Sakata sand dunes (Niigata). Distinction between true 砂丘 and sandy beaches. Specificity of dune descriptions.
improvement_hint: Add dune_type field distinguishing coastal_dune from beach; add Nakatajima Shako sand dunes (Shizuoka) which are a nationally designated natural monument.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks for traditional crafts in the San'in region. Response returns 4 Shimane METI crafts in Chinese: Iwami Yaki (ceramics), Sekishu Washi (paper), Unshuu Soroban (abacus), Izumo Ishi-Touro (stone lantern)—all correctly attributed to Shimane with Chinese descriptions translated at high confidence. Wikidata venue cross-links also present. Excellent coverage and localization quality.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts (San'in also includes Tottori: Inshuu Washi). Only one San'in prefecture covered.
improvement_hint: San'in region queries should call both Shimane and Tottori; consider adding a region-level craft aggregation endpoint or suggest multi-prefecture call.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields beyond Furano. Response returns general Hokkaido spots: Showa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle, Daisetsuzan—none specifically lavender-related. The tool get_spots does not filter by lavender. No lavender_field kind tag appears. The 'beyond Furano' constraint is not meaningfully addressed since Furano (the famous one) isn't even present to contrast with alternatives.
missing_data: Lavender field spots: Furano (baseline), Kamifurano, Nakafurano (Farm Tomita), Biei flower fields. Lavender/flower kind tag in spots. Seasonal bloom data.
improvement_hint: Add flower_field and lavender kind tags to relevant Hokkaido spots (Farm Tomita already in Wikidata Q12024424); search_semantic would handle 'lavender' better than get_spots here.

reasoning: Query in Filipino asks for traditional townscapes (古い町並み) in Kyushu. Response returns Oita prefecture spots: Usuki Castle, Usa Jingu, Oka Castle—historic sites but not specifically preserved old townscapes. Usuki has a notable historic preservation district (Nioza-machi) but this is not surfaced. Kyushu old town districts like Yanagawa (Fukuoka), Asakura, Hita (Oita) are not visible.
missing_data: Yanagawa (canal town), Hita (Mameda historic district), Usuki Stone Buddhas + preservation district, Chiran (already in L2-16). Preservation district / jokamachi kind filter.
improvement_hint: When query is about old townscapes, filter by preservation_district / jokamachi / kominka kinds across multiple Kyushu prefectures. Hita Mameda-machi (重要伝統的建造物群保存地区) is missing.

reasoning: Query asks for kominka stays in the Hokuriku region. Response returns Ishikawa hotels: Hito-yu-kai onsen ryokan (Hakusan area), and city hotels. Bed and Craft kominka network (already seen in spots) appears as a spot in L2-20 but not in hotels. Response doesn't surface kominka_stay type hotels well—OSM entries include unnamed 'Student housing 1' which is noise. Good rural onsen coverage but kominka stays specifically are not well represented.
missing_data: Bed and Craft kominka stays in Nanto/Inami (Toyama). Specific kominka_stay hotels in Kanazawa area (Higashi Chaya district). Fukui, Toyama also missing.
wrong_data: 'Student housing 1' entry (OSM node) is clearly irrelevant noise in accommodation results.
improvement_hint: Filter get_hotels by lodging_type=kominka_stay or traditional_lodging; remove non-tourism OSM entries (student housing, unnamed). Add Toyama/Fukui for Hokuriku-wide coverage.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines in the Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage returns 7 Wakayama items including Kongobu-ji (Koyasan), Kumano Kodo (pilgrimage route, UNESCO WHS), Kumano Sanzan (three grand shrines)—exactly the core Kii mountain shrines. Heritage designations are accurate (UNESCO, Historic Site). The tool_scope_note correctly guides to see_also for additional UNESCO heritage. Excellent coverage of the spiritual/sacred mountain landscape.
missing_data: Yoshino-Omine (Nara side of Kii) mountain shrines. Nachi Taisha specifically named. Full Kumano Sanzan individual shrines (Hongu, Nachi, Hayatama).
improvement_hint: Add Nara prefecture results for cross-prefecture Kii queries (Yoshino-Omine, Dorogawa Onsen area); individual Kumano Sanzan shrine entries could be more granular.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks along the Sea of Japan coast. get_festivals for Niigata returns 129 items. Nagaoka Fireworks Festival (長岡まつり大花火大会) does appear as the 3rd item with August dates—excellent. However, the first two items are spring flower festivals (芝桜, ヒメサユリ) irrelevant to fireworks. 129 items with no fireworks-first ranking means the agent must dig through noise. Omagari Fireworks (Akita) and Nishimonai Festival are not shown.
missing_data: Ranking/filtering for fireworks-type events. Other Sea of Japan prefecture festivals (Akita Omagari, Toyama, Ishikawa summer fireworks). Month-based filtering for summer.
improvement_hint: Add event_type='fireworks_festival' category or kinds tag; allow filtering get_festivals by event_type. Summer fireworks queries should rank 花火 events first.

reasoning: Query asks for bamboo crafts in Oita. Response returns 2 craft items: Hakata Ori (weaving, multi-prefecture including Oita) and Beppu Bamboo Crafts—the latter being exactly the famous Oita bamboo craft with detailed description, production areas (Beppu City, Oita City), and official source. Count is low (only 2) but both are relevant. Beppu Bamboo Crafts has a rich English description and high-confidence translation. See_also venues add museum context.
missing_data: Workshop venues for Beppu bamboo experience (Beppu Traditional Bamboo Crafts Center). Hakata Ori listing may surprise if user expected only Oita-specific crafts.
improvement_hint: Add primary_prefecture flag to craft records to distinguish Oita-primary (Beppu Bamboo) vs cross-prefecture (Hakata Ori); add craft workshop venue links from Wikidata.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Response returns Ehime spots: Ozu Castle, Yuzuki Castle, Omishima Island (cycling_route kind present), Uwajima Castle. Omishima has cycling_route kind tag which is directly relevant and one of the Shimanami Kaido islands. However, the Shimanami Kaido cycling route itself (全国有名サイクリングルート) is not explicitly surfaced. Rural cycling constraint mostly unmet—castle-heavy results.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaido cycling route as a named entity. Rural cycling spots in Kochi, Tokushima. Cycling_route kind filter. Shikoku Pilgrimage cycling option.
improvement_hint: search_hybrid or search_semantic on 'サイクリングロード Shikoku' would retrieve better results; add explicit shimanami_kaido tag and national cycling route entries to spots.

reasoning: Query asks WHERE to see kabuki outside Tokyo/Kyoto—implies venue information. get_traditional_arts returns UNESCO ICH list (183 items) starting with Bunraku, Kabuki (art form, not a venue), Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku—these are art form designations, not performance venues. No venue addresses, theater names, or performance schedules. The constraint 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto' cannot be evaluated from art form records. Rural kabuki venues (Imbari, Chichibu, Awa) not covered.
missing_data: Rural kabuki venues: Kanamaru-za (Kotohira, Kagawa), Awa Jurobe-yashiki (Tokushima puppet kabuki), Chichibu Yomatsuri kabuki. Performance schedules and venue addresses.
improvement_hint: For 'where to see X performance' queries, complement get_traditional_arts with search_area for kabuki theater venues; add venue records for rural performance halls with kabuki_venue kind tag.
